The ethics of new minds · 21st c.

Moral Status & AI Ethics

The ethics of mind extends beyond AI to animals, uncertain cases, and strange forms of subjectivity. This page gathers thinkers who ask when sentience matters, how to reason under uncertainty, and what moral concern is owed to minds unlike our own.

contemporary

Jonathan Birch

Sentience, welfare, and precaution at the edge of minds.

contemporary

Patrick Butlin

Indicator properties for AI consciousness: a framework for empirical assessment.

contemporary

Lucius Caviola

Psychology of moral circle expansion and attitudes toward artificial minds.

contemporary

Robert Long

Digital minds as moral patients: taking AI welfare seriously.

contemporary

Bradford Saad

Uncertainty, moral weight, and the governance of emerging artificial minds.

contemporary

Eric Schwitzgebel

Introspective unreliability, weird minds, and the ethics of designing experience.

contemporary

Jeff Sebo

Moral circle, animals, and the ethics of minds we do not yet understand.

contemporary

Kristin Andrews

Animal minds, social cognition, and ethics under uncertainty about consciousness.

b. 1947

Martha Nussbaum

Capabilities, emotion, and the ethical significance of vulnerable minds.

Digital Minds · A history of thinking about the mind, from antiquity to the present day.
A personal research project by Fabián. · About · Glossary
